title: Black fig and tea soy candle
subtitle: A container soy candle scented with a fragrance blend of dark fig, black tea, and warm amber
excerpt: This container candle uses a dark, complex fragrance combining sweet fig, black tea, and amber base notes. Poured into a dark glass jar, it suits an evening or study setting. Standard container soy wax, fragrance at 8%, single cotton wick.
sourceNotes: Soy container wax formulation and pour temperature: Candlescience technical guide (2022). Fragrance load at 8% (within soy wax maximum): Bramble Berry candle guide (2023). IFRA guidelines for fragrance oil in container candles: category 12 limits apply.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale wax and fragrance proportionally. Pour multiple jars from the same batch for consistency.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Soy candles improve with a cure of 48 hours to a week after pouring; the fragrance throw develops fully during this time.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: The candle keeps several years. Fragrance is strongest in the first two years. Keep the lid on when not in use to slow fragrance loss.

glossaryTerms.definition: The first time a candle is lit, burn it long enough for the wax pool to reach the full diameter of the jar. This sets the burn pattern and prevents tunnelling.
The temperature at which wax is poured into the container. Soy container wax pours at 60 to 65 degrees C for the best adhesion and finish.

content.text: A container soy candle built around a fig-and-tea fragrance blend. Dark fig brings sweetness and depth; black tea adds a dry, tannic note; amber rounds out the base. Poured in a dark glass jar, it suits an evening room or a desk. The process is the same as any soy container candle: melt, cool, fragrance, pour at the right temperature, leave to set.

items.prepNote: soft container grade, e.g. 464 or equivalent
8% of wax weight; ensure fragrance oil is rated for candle use
ECO-4 or CD-12 for a 6 to 7 cm jar diameter

content.text: Melt wax over a water bath only, never directly on the hob. Never leave melting wax unattended; hot wax above its flash point catches like an oil fire. Keep a kitchen fire blanket within reach.
Melt the soy wax over a water bath to 75 degrees C. Remove from heat and let it cool to 60 to 65 degrees C. Stir in the fragrance oil and mix for 2 minutes.
Centre the pre-tabbed wick with a wick bar. Pour the wax at the
of 60 to 65 degrees C. Leave undisturbed at room temperature for at least 8 hours, then trim the wick to 5 mm.
rule: burn until the wax pool reaches the full diameter of the jar, around 2 to 3 hours for a 6 cm jar. Extinguishing before this point creates a tunnel that deepens on every subsequent burn.
Replace the lid after each burn to slow fragrance loss. Store away from heat and direct light. The candle keeps several years; fragrance is strongest in the first two years.
